title: Gotten sick
wikitext:
[]: Gotten sick?             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Fri Sep 02, 2005 10:00 am Has anyone goten sick of watching eva? I was trying to watch EoE last night and i just couldnt care less about it.... I just shut it off after the half way point. I know I'm in the tripple didgits for watching it for sure but now im so sick of it. Is this a bad thing?!? or do I need an eva break? Anyways is anyone else tired of any part of eva whether it be a episode or movie?

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Fri Sep 02, 2005 10:12 am Of course. Too much of anything will cause boredom or even hatred. There are other anime or even things outside of anime worth doing. It's all about balance. I caught EoE on the big screen recently but I haven't watched it in a while so it was refreshing for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Sep 02, 2005 2:23 pm There isn't a single episode that I can sit though anymore. It feels like I'm staring at the wall.

[]:             Quote Post by Machinehead » Fri Sep 02, 2005 5:36 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: There isn't a single episode that I can sit though anymore. It feels like I'm staring at the wall.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Sep 02, 2005 5:41 pm Well, without exaggerating, I've probably seen Evangelion in its entirety between 20 and 30 times. I'm actually glad I don't want to see it anymore--that'd just be more time wasted. *continues wandering the internet aimlessly*

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Sep 02, 2005 5:42 pm Dont yo know watching eva caused cancer?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 02, 2005 6:07 pm I don't watch episodes in their entirety anymore unless I'm showing NGE to somebody. Otherwise, I "watch" 'em because forum antics or my baby (FGC) demand it. The end of episode #19 still gives me the shivers, though. :barely audible moan:

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Sep 02, 2005 11:51 pm Hehehe, not having money for imports and living somewhere without local editions doesn't feel so bad right now.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sun Sep 04, 2005 9:12 am DatDude wrote: Dont yo know watching eva caused cancer?

[]: nah not me             Quote Post by JinKazama » Thu Sep 08, 2005 5:45 am first time watching Eva was in d middle of this year, after that for different reasons I've watched it serval times more(rewatch in sub/dub, and once had to guide my friend in watching it), till now haven't been in contact with any Eva stuff for a few months I guess, somehow now I've been obcessed with flightsims but the longer it has been as if I've been trying to forget eva in a way or another, the more the urge I'll have wanting to watch again whenever on a random thought of any eva stuff. Nonethelsss, the strong feel and amazement will never return after the first time it has been watched though, but those rest in mind, and makes you want to look back into it sometime later.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Thu Sep 08, 2005 3:46 pm Well, I haven't gotten to that point yet., and I usually dont until I find something new, but now I have discovered an amazing aspect that can improve your life slightly if you enjoy doing it! A JOB O.o Free food in tow, too. Booya! and, to sachiel. sachiel wrote: I get sick of a few episodes, especially JA, because I hated that thing. But I never really get sick of watching EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Magami no D00M » Sat Sep 10, 2005 1:41 pm DatDude wrote: Dont yo know watching eva caused cancer?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sat Sep 10, 2005 1:58 pm Wow you guys have watched it a lot. In its entirety I've probably watched it around ten times at the most. I can't say I've really gotten tired of it, but usually my viewings of it are pretty far spaced out.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Sat Sep 10, 2005 5:21 pm Magami no D00M wrote: I prfer not watching EOE in it's entirety anymore, I usually like to start around where Asuka gets eaten. Because teh begining has just gotten boring for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Sep 10, 2005 5:22 pm There was a point where I just had to watch Evangelion every day for about three months--maybe longer. If it wasn't watching it, then it was reading articles or fanfiction. In truth, a part of me hates this fucking show.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Sat Sep 10, 2005 5:54 pm It does get boring after a while, doesn't it. Even the fanfiction some times does, a lot of good fanfic writers either have or have stopped writting fanfiction for eva. Part of me wishes that i had discovered Eva sooner, another wishes that i never did.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Sat Sep 10, 2005 6:25 pm I always end up having a mental breakdown, too, when I get bored of anime of some sorts. I have major obsession problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Sep 10, 2005 8:29 pm Lately I've been on a kind of Love Hina kick. I can't think of any particular reason why that manga appeals to me so much, but it just does. Lately I've even become more tolerant of the anime. For some inexplicable (and likely frightening) reason, I've even been compelled to drown myself in the music of said anime, and those who know me know that when I drown myself in the sonic residue of any particular artist or artwork, I begin to imagine even more of whatever world I'm consuming. This doesn't even begin touch the height of my Eva obsession. I would say that it never got boring--I just needed some space. And there are those with whom you can't simply have space; you'll have to wait at least several years before you ever rediscover terms that are nearly as sweet. When dealing with a limited world of fiction, the honeymoon ends a lot sooner that one would expect at the climax of the affair. Removing those limits just leaves you in another finite space with finitely-abled writers. At some point you just put it away. And wait.

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Wed Sep 14, 2005 2:43 am if anything, the action arc (everything up until #16) tends to be a chore to watch. now, i realize the value of a little comic relief, but Asuka's introductory episodes (especially 'both of you, dance like you want to win!') are a tad too hokey-jokey.

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Sep 14, 2005 8:48 am Hexon.Arq wrote: Lately I've been on a kind of Love Hina kick. I can't think of any particular reason why that manga appeals to me so much, but it just does. Lately I've even become more tolerant of the anime. For some inexplicable (and likely frightening) reason, I've even been compelled to drown myself in the music of said anime, and those who know me know that when I drown myself in the sonic residue of any particular artist or artwork, I begin to imagine even more of whatever world I'm consuming.
